theatres.name: Iran / Strait of Hormuz
theatres.summary: Day 30 — war reaches one-month mark. MAJOR DEVELOPMENTS: (1) HOUTHIS ENTER THE WAR — fired ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since conflict began, targeting 'sensitive military sites' in southern Israel; air raid sirens in Beersheba; both missiles intercepted. Houthis vow to continue strikes. (2) USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU (~3,500 sailors and Marines) CONFIRMED ARRIVED in CENTCOM area of responsibility (Mar 27-28) — first amphibious assault force in theatre. (3) Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia injured 10-15 US troops (5 seriously); 2+ USAF refueling aircraft and an E-3 AWACS damaged; US wounded in war now 300+. (4) Pentagon weighing up to 10,000 additional ground troops for Middle East (Axios, Fox News). (5) Rubio told G7 in France: war to end in 'weeks, not months'; said no ground troops needed but deploying them for 'maximum optionality.' (6) Heavy bombardment continues across Tehran — strikes in northeastern, western, central, and eastern parts of city. (7) Oil surged to ~$112.57/bbl (+4.2% on Mar 28). (8) WHO: 9 paramedics killed in Lebanon on Saturday (Mar 29) — 51 health workers killed in March. (9) Iran death toll: ~1,900-2,000+ (Al Jazeera); US KIA 13; US wounded 300+; Lebanon: 1,189+ killed. (10) China launched 2 reciprocal trade investigations against US (Mar 27) ahead of May summit. Apr 6 energy strike deadline remains key decision point.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.summary: Russia holds ~20% of Ukraine. Russian forces captured Brusovka in DPR on Mar 28 — forming southern envelopment of Stary Karavan on the Liman-Slavyansk railway. Preparing bridgeheads for operation against Slavyansk fortified hub. Combat engagements continuing decline from 176 peak (Mar 25). 45 artillery and drone attacks hit Dnipropetrovsk region on Mar 28 afternoon; Kryvyi Rih industrial facility hit; drone attack injured man in Kyiv's Brovary district. Russia lost 4 sq mi during week of Mar 17-24 — shift from earlier momentum. ISW: mechanized assaults increasing since Mar 17, spring-summer offensive preparations against Fortress Belt in Donetsk. US-Ukraine bilateral talks held in Miami Mar 21-22 — 'constructive' but Russia NOT present. Abu Dhabi trilateral talks still postponed. Iran war continues to divert attention and boost Russian war economy via energy prices. Record 67% of Russians support peace negotiations.

theatres.name: Latin America — Venezuela / Cuba / Colombia
theatres.summary: Maduro court hearing held Mar 26. Judge Hellerstein did NOT dismiss charges but did NOT rule on defense funding issue either — promised decision 'soon' on whether to order Trump admin to permit Venezuela to fund Maduro's defense. Said if government refuses to comply with any such order, he would then entertain dismissal. Hellerstein emphasized 6th Amendment right to counsel is 'paramount.' No timeline or next hearing date set. Rodriguez acting president — oil reform, amnesty. US recognized Rodriguez Mar 11; diplomatic relations restored Mar 5. Cuba crisis deepening: second nationwide blackout in a week (Mar 22-23); government released 51 political prisoners in 'goodwill' gesture following Holy See engagement; daily protests since Mar 6 — Cubalex documented 156+ protests and 47+ arrests by Mar 17. Secret US-Cuba talks ongoing. Colombia in economic emergency; peace process collapsed.

theatres.name: US-China Trade
theatres.summary: Trump-Xi summit rescheduled to May 14-15 in Beijing (previously postponed indefinitely). China launched 2 reciprocal trade investigations against US on Mar 27 — examining US restrictions on Chinese goods, export controls on advanced tech, and barriers to green energy exports; 6-month deadline with 3-month extension possible; legal groundwork for countermeasures ahead of summit. US-Taiwan Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) signed Feb 12 — eliminates/reduces tariffs on up to 99% of goods. Paris trade talks (Mar 15-16) were 'very good' per Bessent. US intelligence assesses China NOT planning Taiwan invasion in 2027 but is closely studying Iran war for lessons. Section 301 investigations ongoing against China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, EU. China's 2026 defense budget up 7% to ~$278B.

theatres.name: East Asia — DPRK / Korean Peninsula
theatres.summary: No major new developments Mar 29. Trump expressed interest in meeting Kim Jong Un when visiting China for May summit, but unlikely as US hasn't changed North Korea policy to meet Kim's requirements. North Korea continues prioritizing military ties with Russia over economic ties with China; remains unmoved by South Korean President Lee Jae-myung's signals for reduced inter-Korean tensions. Lukashenko completed first-ever visit to DPRK Mar 25-26; friendship treaty signed. Kim invoked Iran war to justify nuclear weapons, declared nuclear status 'irreversible.' No missile launches since Mar 14. Russia-DPRK alliance deepened; 12,000 DPRK troops in Russia.

ground_invasion_tracker.draft_indicators: None. No legislation introduced. No Selective Service activation. No public discussion at Pentagon or White House level. Rubio explicitly stated no ground troops needed. However, Pentagon weighing 10,000 additional troops including infantry and armored vehicles — a significant qualitative shift if approved.
ground_invasion_tracker.assessment: Status remains 'staging' but CAPABILITY IN THEATRE for the first time. USS Tripoli arrival gives the US its first amphibious assault capability in the war zone. The Pentagon's consideration of 10,000 additional troops (infantry + armor) is the most significant escalation signal yet — it would more than double the ground-capable force. However, Rubio's public messaging ('weeks not months,' no ground troops needed) and the ongoing diplomatic track create a counternarrative. The Prince Sultan Air Base attack — injuring 10-15 troops and damaging aircraft — could become a political catalyst either for escalation (retaliation) or de-escalation (political cost). Houthi entry into the war adds a new front and complicates Hormuz resolution. The Apr 6 deadline is now 8 days away. USS Boxer's mid-April arrival timeline and the 10,000-troop decision could converge near the deadline. Assessment: the situation is transitioning from 'staging' toward 'partial capability in place' — the US now has forces that COULD conduct limited operations (island seizure, maritime interdiction) but NOT a major ground invasion. The next week will determine whether the diplomatic or military track prevails.

daily_entries.prediction_impact: Houthi entry into the war is the most significant escalation since the energy-infrastructure warfare spiral of Mar 18-19. After nearly 4 weeks of 'strategic patience,' the Houthis launching missiles at Israel signals either: (1) Tehran coordinated activation of another front, or (2) Houthis acting independently as diplomatic talks stall. WashPost warns of renewed Red Sea shipping disruption — this would compound the Hormuz crisis. The Prince Sultan Air Base attack (10-15 US wounded, aircraft damaged) is politically significant — the rising US casualty count (300+) and damage to high-value assets may increase domestic pressure. Rubio's 'weeks not months' messaging at G7 may be managing expectations or signaling imminent escalation. The Pentagon's consideration of 10,000 more troops contradicts the 'no ground troops' rhetoric.

daily_entries.summary: Day 29 of war. Israel struck 2 nuclear facilities — Arak heavy water complex (Shahid Khondab) and Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd — and bombed 2 IRGC-linked steel plants (Khuzestan Steel near Ahvaz, Mobarakeh Steel in Isfahan). Mobarakeh is the largest steel company in MENA, responsible for ~1% of Iran's GDP and >50% of national steel production — strike expected to 'paralyze' Iran's steel industry and cause billions in damage. Defense Minister Katz vowed to 'intensify and expand' strikes. IRGC Aerospace commander Brig. Gen. Mousavi warned workers at US/Israeli-linked sites to evacuate, threatening retaliation 'beyond eye for eye.' Iran's Atomic Energy Organization confirmed strikes on Arak and Ardakan but said no casualties or radiation release at nuclear sites. Casualties: 1 killed + 16 injured at Mobarakeh Steel; 16 minor injuries at Khuzestan Steel. In Israel, cluster munitions killed 1 man (60s) in Tel Aviv. Witkoff said he expects meetings with Iran 'this week' and noted 'ships are passing — very good sign.' Possible Islamabad meeting being arranged. Oil at ~$107.81/bbl. Updated Iran death toll: ~1,937 (Al Jazeera tracker).

daily_entries.prediction_impact: Israel's escalation to industrial/economic targets (steel plants = ~1% of GDP) and additional nuclear facilities represents a significant widening of the target set — moving beyond purely military objectives to systematic economic destruction. The IRGC's evacuation warning and 'beyond eye for eye' rhetoric suggests Iran may retaliate against US-linked industrial sites in Gulf states — a potential escalation that could further destabilize regional energy infrastructure. Witkoff's optimism about 'this week' meetings exists in tension with Israel's vowed escalation. The US acknowledgment that war may exceed 4-6 weeks signals the air campaign alone is insufficient to achieve objectives.

White House insists talks remain 'productive'; press secretary Leavitt claimed negotiations continuing despite rejection. Trump vowed to 'hit harder.' The 5-day postponement deadline is now ~2 days away (Mar 28). Iran struck Kuwait International Airport with drones, hitting fuel tank and sparking massive fire (limited damage, no casualties). Kuwait army shot down 6 drones and 5 unmanned aircraft.

The expansion of the conflict into a full-scale Lebanon ground operation with annexation ambitions represents a parallel ground war front that was not part of the 'Iran trap' thesis but adds to the broader regional conflagration.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, Axios, HRW, Haaretz, Democracy Now, The Defense Post

daily_entries.summary: 168 combat engagements recorded on Mar 24, up from 161 on Mar 21. Greatest Russian pressure on Pokrovsk sector; increased activity in Kostiantynivka and Huliaipole sectors. Russia lost 890 soldiers in 24 hours per Ukraine. 17 attacks near Kostiantynivka area alone. Record 67% of Russians now support peace negotiations. Zelenskyy says Ukraine waiting on US and Russia to set next trilateral round.

Kostiantynivka: 17 attacks near Pleshchiivka, Illinivka, Kleban-Byk, Sofiivka, Ivanopillia

Abu Dhabi trilateral talks still postponed due to Iran war
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The Ukraine war grinds on with increasing Russian losses but continued pressure on Ukrainian positions. The record high in Russian public support for peace negotiations (67%) is a notable shift. However, the Iran war continues to consume US diplomatic bandwidth, delaying any progress.

daily_entries.summary: Nuclear-facility tit-for-tat escalation marks new phase of war. US struck Iran's Natanz nuclear enrichment complex again with bunker busters (Mar 22); Iran retaliated with missiles on Dimona and Arad near Israel's Negev Nuclear Research Center, wounding 180+ — first successful penetration of Israeli air defenses in that area. Israeli military acknowledged interceptors 'failed to hit the threats.' IAEA says no radiation damage at either site. Israel struck Tehran again on Mar 23 with explosions in east of city. Iran intercepted a US-Israeli armed drone over Tehran.
details: US struck Natanz with bunker busters on Mar 22; IDF denied Israeli involvement
Iran framed Dimona/Arad strikes as 'response' to Natanz attack — nuclear tit-for-tat
116 wounded in Arad (7 serious), 64 wounded in Dimona (1 serious) — 180 total
Israeli firefighters: 'interceptors were launched that failed to hit the threats, resulting in two direct hits by ballistic missiles with warheads weighing hundreds of kilograms'
IAEA: no abnormal radiation levels detected at either Dimona or Natanz
Iran's Tasnim news agency: armed drone intercepted over Tehran airspace
Israel launched new strikes on east Tehran on Mar 23
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The nuclear-facility tit-for-tat represents a dangerous escalation — both sides are now targeting each other's nuclear infrastructure. Israeli air defense failure at Dimona is significant; if Iranian missiles can penetrate defenses near Israel's most sensitive nuclear site, the calculus for continued escalation changes.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CBS News, NPR, BusinessToday, Euronews, Military.com, The Week, IAEA

daily_entries.summary: Iran fired 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean ~4,000km from Iran. One malfunctioned in flight, one was intercepted. No damage to base. UK confirmed the attack and condemned it as 'reckless.' This reveals Iran possesses 4,000km+ missile capability — previously assessed at 2,000km maximum. UK agreed to allow US to use British military bases for Hormuz strikes.

Base can host B-2 stealth bombers; strategic staging area for US operations
UK MoD condemned 'reckless attacks'; confirmed both missiles failed to reach target
Analysts: 'These missiles mean Iran has 4,000km-plus ballistic missiles — not revealed before'
UK decision to allow US use of British bases for Hormuz operations came same day
Diego Garcia strike means European capitals are theoretically within Iranian missile range
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Iran's demonstrated 4,000km missile range is a major intelligence surprise. If Iran can strike Diego Garcia, it can reach parts of southern Europe. This fundamentally changes the threat calculus for European nations and could accelerate European defense spending — supporting Jiang's broader thesis about European rearmament.
daily_entries.source: Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, CNN, CNBC, Euronews, ITV News, Military.com, Fox News

daily_entries.summary: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum on Hormuz expires today (Mar 23). Iran 'unswayed' per NBC — no compliance expected. Iran's IRGC says if power plants hit, Hormuz will be 'completely closed' and won't reopen until plants are rebuilt. USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU expected to arrive in theatre Mar 23-27. CENTCOM confirms ~50,000 US troops now in Middle East. Trump gave contradictory signals: said considering 'winding down' (Mar 21) but then issued ultimatum same day. 82nd Airborne mentioned in Pentagon ground operation plans.

Iran IRGC: 'Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed, and it will not be reopened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt'
CENTCOM: ~50,000 US troops in Middle East theatre
USS Tripoli ESG arriving imminently — composition: Tripoli (LHA-7), Robert Smalls (CG), Rafael Peralta (DDG), 31st MEU
Defence Security Asia: Pentagon plans include 82nd Airborne in addition to Marine units
Trump on 'winding down': told reporters considering it, but ultimatum contradicts
Admin telling foreign officials: Trump-Xi summit won't happen until Iran war ends
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The ultimatum deadline expiring without Iranian compliance creates a binary decision point: Trump either follows through (striking power plants, triggering full Hormuz closure and Gulf infrastructure retaliation) or backs down (damaging credibility). Either outcome is escalatory. The arrival of the Tripoli ARG with amphibious-capable Marines coincides with this decision point — Kharg Island seizure remains the most likely ground operation if escalation continues.
daily_entries.source: NBC News, USNI News, Axios, Military.com, CBS News, Defence Security Asia, Political Wire

Witkoff called talks 'constructive' and said they 'focused on narrowing and resolving remaining items.' Notably, Russia was NOT present — this was bilateral only. Last trilateral talks were Geneva Feb 17-18. On the frontline, Russia advancing on Kostiantynivka and toward Kramatorsk/Sloviansk.

daily_entries.summary: Trump-Xi summit postponed indefinitely. Originally scheduled Mar 31-Apr 2 in Beijing; Trump said delay 'a month or so' due to Iran war, later said 'reset' to mid-May. Saudi oil tanker with 1M barrels destined for India also reportedly allowed through
IRGC Deputy FM: 'Countries that joined the aggression should not benefit from safe passage'

38 North analysis: DPRK using Iran war moment to assert regional presence
